name: Find The Array
input:
The first line contains one integer $t$ ($1 \le t \le 1000$) — the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains one integer $n$ ($2 \le n \le 50$).
The second line contains $n$ integers $a_1, a_2, \dots, a_n$ ($1 \le a_i \le 10^9$).
output: For each test case, print the beautiful array $b_1, b_2, \dots, b_n$ ($1 \le b_i \le 10^9$) on a separate line. It can be shown that at least one beautiful array exists under these circumstances. If there are multiple answers, print any of them.
